questionHtml:
What is the enthalpy change of the reaction, in kJ?

2C (graphite) + O2 (g) → 2CO (g)

A.  −394 − 283

B.  2(−394) + 2(−283)

C.  −394 + 283

D.  2(−394) + 2(283)

meta.title: What is the enthalpy change of the reaction, in kJ? 2C (graphite) + O2 (g) → 2CO (g) A. −394 − 283 B. 2(−394) + 2(−283) C. −394 + 283 D. 2(−394) + 2(283)
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.2.2—Hess’s law states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states. Apply Hess’s law to calculate enthalpy changes in multistep reactions.
